items.title: I just made $25K USD with my capybara game built with Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: Someone built a capybara game using Claude Code and made $25K USD. This is exactly the kind of shipping story that moves you from reading to building, showing real revenue generation from a project built with your primary AI coding tool. You can study their approach and launch thread for insights into shipping and monetizing with Claude Code.

items.title: Show HN: My AI agent has 1 hour to earn €10 – it shipped this page itself
items.why_it_matters: An AI agent was tasked to earn €10 in one hour and successfully shipped a web page itself. This is a concrete example of an AI agent taking action and delivering a tangible output, which is key to learning how to direct AI effectively. You can investigate how this agent was structured and what tools it used to 'ship' the page, then apply similar principles to automate parts of your AI-ingest project.

items.title: Show HN: Persistent Claude Code sessions you drive as a terminal or a chat
items.why_it_matters: A new tool, Agentpeek, offers persistent Claude Code sessions that you can drive via terminal or chat. This directly enhances your primary coding environment by making your interaction with Claude Code more consistent and powerful. You can try integrating Agentpeek into your Claude Code workflow this week to see if it improves your ability to direct the AI and manage sessions for your AI-ingest project.

items.title: Show HN: Makoto the Claude Code plugin that keeps the AI honest
items.why_it_matters: Makoto is a new Claude Code plugin designed to help keep the AI honest and verify its outputs. This directly addresses the challenge of knowing what to verify and what to keep when directing AI, making your 'vibe coding' more reliable. You can try using Makoto with Claude Code on a specific task in your AI-ingest project to evaluate its effectiveness in verifying AI-generated code or responses.

items.title: GPT-5.6, Grok 4.5, Claude, and Muse Spark build the same 4 apps
items.why_it_matters: A new comparison shows how GPT-5.6 (Luna, Terra, Sol), Grok 4.5, Claude, and Muse Spark perform when building the same four applications. This is a practical benchmark that includes Claude, giving you insight into its real-world building capabilities compared to other major models. You can study the examples of the apps built by Claude in this comparison and try to replicate one of them using Claude Code for your own learning, or apply the observed best practices to your AI-ingest project.

items.title: One Wikipedia page costs your AI agent 68,000 tokens
items.why_it_matters: Research shows that fetching a single Wikipedia page can cost your AI agent 68,000 tokens in raw HTML, even though Claude Code's built-in webfetch can summarize it to around 950 tokens. This highlights a critical cost consideration for your AI-ingest project, which likely involves web scraping. You can use this information to be more mindful of token usage when directing Claude Code to fetch web content, and explore more targeted scraping or pre-processing for your AI-ingest project to save costs.

items.title: Claude Savings with context caching awareness
items.why_it_matters: A new open-source library, Brick-SR1, aims to reduce Claude API costs through context caching awareness. Since you use Claude heavily, optimizing token usage is directly relevant to your project's economic viability. You can investigate this library to see if you can integrate context caching into your Claude API calls for the AI-ingest project to reduce token usage and costs.

items.title: Show HN: Token Governor – The cheapest LLM token is the one you never send
items.why_it_matters: Token Governor is a new tool that helps optimize LLM token usage by preventing unnecessary sends, based on the principle that 'the cheapest LLM token is the one you never send.' This is a practical way to reduce API costs for your AI-ingest project, aligning with your interest in cost savings. You can explore integrating Token Governor into your AI-ingest project to optimize your LLM calls and reduce API costs this week.

items.title: Crawl4AI: Open-source web crawler and scraper for LLMs
items.why_it_matters: Crawl4AI is an open-source web crawler and scraper specifically designed for LLMs. Given your AI-ingest project's goal of filtering AI news from sources like Twitter, a dedicated web scraping tool is highly relevant for data collection. You can try integrating Crawl4AI into your AI-ingest project to improve its data collection capabilities for filtering AI news, potentially making your ingestion process more efficient.

items.title: Show HN: R3 – A Local Code Review Tool for You and Your AI Agent
items.why_it_matters: R3 is a new local code review tool designed for you and your AI agent, helping organize feedback across documents. As you're learning Git/GitHub and aiming to improve your direction of AI, a structured feedback tool can be very beneficial. You can try using R3 to review some of the code generated by Claude Code for your AI-ingest project, focusing on giving more structured and trackable feedback to the AI.

items.title: I made a Pirate MMO with Fable and the game is only 5MB
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer built a Pirate MMO game with Fable that is only 5MB in size. While not using your exact stack, this is an inspiring example of someone shipping a complex project with AI and achieving impressive technical efficiency. You can look into the project's development process and the choices made to achieve such a small file size, applying any relevant lessons to optimize your own project's performance.

items.title: Sol 5.6 on $20 Codex did 2.2x more average tasks than Fable 5 on $20 ClaudeCode
items.why_it_matters: A benchmark comparison shows Sol 5.6 on Codex performed 2.2x more average tasks than Fable 5 on ClaudeCode for $20. This provides a practical comparison of the efficiency of your primary AI coding tools for specific tasks. You could experiment with using Codex for specific coding tasks within your AI-ingest project to see if you can achieve similar efficiency gains, potentially saving time or costs.

items.title: Record before and after clips for pull request using Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: A new tool helps record before-and-after clips for pull requests using Claude Code. As you're learning Git and GitHub, this can help improve your commit discipline and make your pull requests clearer. You could explore using this tool for your next pull request on the AI-ingest project to enhance your code review process and communication.

items.title: Show HN: 9lives – Self-healing test runner that refuses to mask real bugs
items.why_it_matters: 9lives is a self-healing test runner designed to prevent coding agents from breaking tests in trivial ways, especially for Playwright. While you're a beginner, learning good testing practices is crucial, and this tool addresses a common pain point when using AI for coding. You could explore integrating 9lives into your AI-ingest project's testing setup to make your tests more robust against AI-induced changes, especially if you start using Playwright for frontend testing.
